report:
The overall data quality of the dataset is relatively good, with no duplicates found. However, there are some missing values, with the Education column having the highest number of missing values at 5, followed by Age and Income with 3 and 2 missing values respectively. The presence of missing values may impact the accuracy of any models built using this data. Additionally, there are some outliers present, particularly in the Income column, where 12 data points fall outside the lower and upper bounds. The Age column also has one outlier, while the Target column has 6 outliers, although these are all at the lower bound of 0.

The correlations between columns are relatively weak, with the strongest correlation being between Age and Income at 0.2127. The correlations between the other columns are all below 0.15, suggesting that there are no strong relationships between these variables. The distribution of the categorical variables is also worth noting, with the Education column having a clear majority of Bachelor's degrees at 43, followed by High School at 25. The Name column has a more even distribution, with the top 5 names all having between 6 and 8 occurrences.

Before modeling this data, it is worth noting that the Target variable has a highly skewed distribution, with a mean of 0.06 and a standard deviation of 0.2387. This suggests that the Target variable may be binary, with a large majority of 0s and a small minority of 1s. The presence of missing values and outliers in the data may also impact the performance of any models built using this data. Additionally, the weak correlations between columns suggest that a simple linear model may not be sufficient to capture the underlying relationships in the data, and more complex models such as decision trees or random forests may be necessary. Overall, careful data preprocessing and feature engineering will be necessary to build accurate models using this data.

cleaning_plan.notes: The provided dataset profile shows missing values in Age, Income, and Education columns, which will be handled by replacing with median for numeric columns and mode for categorical columns. There are no duplicates, so they will be kept. Outliers are present in Age, Income, and Target columns, and will be handled by removing them from Age and Target, and capping them in Income. Education is a low-cardinality categorical column and will be one-hot encoded.

recommendations.detection_reasoning: Column 'Target' matches a common target-column naming convention. Target 'Target' is numeric but has only 2 unique values, which reads as encoded class labels regardless of dataset size, rather than a continuous quantity.

ranked_models.name: Logistic Regression
ranked_models.reason: The dataset is small (99 rows), where simpler linear models tend to generalize better than tree ensembles that need more data to shine; it's more sensitive to the outliers detected in this dataset.

ranked_models.reason: the small dataset size (99 rows) limits how well it can learn; it's robust to the outliers found in the profile; class distribution is imbalanced (minority/majority ratio 0.06).
